%I A______ (placeholder)

%S 1,4,9,18,35,67,129,249,480,925,1783,3437,6625,12770,24591,47298,
%T 90948,174939,336544

%N BFS layer sizes of the 3D right-corner orthoscheme reflection group with two equal legs and one distinct leg (Class B), computed for legs (1,1,2).

%C The 3D right-corner orthoscheme has three mutually perpendicular legs at a vertex V_0. W = <R_0, R_1, R_2, R_3> is generated by the reflections across the four faces; a(n) is the number of distinct group elements of minimal W-word-length n (the size of BFS layer n in the Cayley graph).

%C Class B denotes the case of exactly two equal legs and one distinct leg. No closed form is known; the sequence appears to exhibit intermediate (non-polynomial, sub-exponential-rate) growth and is not produced by any short linear recurrence.

%C The associated Coxeter diagram is a path on four nodes whose consecutive facet pairs carry label infinity and whose non-consecutive (perpendicular) facet pairs carry label 2 (commuting). Because the orthoscheme is a Euclidean (affine) reflection group whose facet pairs do not all meet at angles that are rational multiples of pi, a(n) is NOT known to coincide with the rational Coxeter (Steinberg-Solomon) Poincare word-growth series of any single fixed Coxeter system; no proven exact rational growth series is available for this sequence. The realized affine growth lies strictly below the free-Coxeter maximum, since the perpendicular facet pairs commute (m=2).

%C Values computed by exact-rational BFS on legs (a,b,c)=(1,1,2) through depth 18 (terms a(0)..a(18)), triple-verified.

%C Leg-independence within Class B is empirically observed, not proven: identical initial terms were obtained by independent BFS on legs (1,1,2), (1,1,3), (2,2,3), (1,1,5) and (3,3,7). Dependence only on the equal/distinct symmetry pattern of the legs is conjectural in this class. (Leg-independence is proven only for Class C, the pairwise-distinct case; the proof there does not transfer, since it relies on all three dihedral angles being irrational multiples of pi, which fails when two legs are equal.)

%C Empirically a(n+1)/a(n) is near 1.925; a(18)/a(17) = 1.9238. No minimal polynomial for the growth rate is known, since no rational generating function is known for this class.

%C Finite-range coincidence: the tetranacci recurrence t(n)=t(n-1)+t(n-2)+t(n-3)+t(n-4) (the recurrence underlying A251704) reproduces a(6) through a(13) but then diverges: it predicts 24615 at n=14 whereas the true value is a(14)=24591. The tetranacci dominant root 1.92756... is therefore a coincidence and is not the true growth rate (the empirical ratio trends below it).

%C Direct linear-algebra tests on the depth-18 data rule out a linear recurrence over Q of order <= 9.

%H V. Bonfioli, <a href="https://github.com/elvec1o/pythagorean-reflection-sequence">Pythagorean reflection sequence (repository)</a>, 2026.

%H V. Bonfioli, <a href="https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20370090">A Universal Right-Triangle Reflection Sequence (Zenodo)</a>, 2026. [Title cited for reference; the "universal"/leg-independence result it establishes applies to the pairwise-distinct case (Class C), not to this Class B sequence.]

%Y Cf. A396406 (2D right-triangle reflection sequence).
%Y Cf. A251704 (tetranacci variant; agrees with this sequence on a(6)..a(13) but diverges at a(14)).

%K nonn,hard,more

%O 0,2

%E More terms welcome; no closed form known.

%A Vico Bonfioli, 2026
